entries.title: Homily on Acts 53 on acts 27:34
entries.excerpt: And how, say you, did they go without food, having taken nothing? How did they bear it? Their fear possessed them, and did not let them fall into a desire of food, being, as they were, at the point of extreme jeopardy; but they had no care for food. Moreover, the place is a trying one, for it was in the Adriatic, and then their long abstinence. They were in the midst of death. It was now the fourteenth day that they were going without food, having taken nothing.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 27:34
entries.excerpt: "For this," he says, "is for your survival," that is, to eat, in case you perish through hunger. For they had no concern for food, since the danger was not about those present. From where then did it reveal to them that the number had increased among those sailing? It was fitting for them to ask the reason for which they sail, and to learn everything.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 27:34
entries.excerpt: "Therefore I insist you to take food." Paul urges them to partake of food, and he himself partakes first, persuading not by words but by deed.
